Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BLUE SKY FOREVER's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

Plaintiff's unopposed Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and Consent Judgment is granted. Plaintiff to submit a proposed order and a Consent Judgment.

Visiting Judge: Cindee Mayfield, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/CM)

Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
Off Calendar Dec-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF BLUE SKY FOREVER's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment.

Plaintiff Blue Sky Forever's unopposed Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and Consent Judgment is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing, Plaintiff's motion to approve settlement pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(f) and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6 is granted. Based on the record before it, the court finds: (1) the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; (2) the penalty amounts are reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health and Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2); and, (3) the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law. The consent judgments shall be entered consistent with the Proposition 65 Settlement and the Parties' [Proposed] Consent Judgment.

Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order quoting the text above verbatim.

Prior to the hearing time, Moving Party must lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a copy of the proposed consent judgment executed by all relevant parties.

No proposed order or proposed judgment received. Moving party failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats the court's ruling verbatim and a proposed judgment executed by all relevant parties and must drop hard courtesy copies in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
